# UC-22.13.4 · Annual CIP-002 Categorization Review Evidence Package

## Description

We timestamp inventory validation searches and signed review workflows so annual categorization review evidence is searchable and complete.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes cip_evidence, itsm and sourcetypes cip:review_ticket, cip:review_ticket`, cip:search_evidence. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Emit HEC when the responsible manager signs the annual review. (2) Schedule the inventory recon search to write `cip:search_evidence` events. (3) Alert if `signed_ok=0` approaching the compliance date. (4) PDF the dashboard for the audit binder. Indexes required: `index=cip_evidence`, `index=itsm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=cip:review_ticket`, `sourcetype=cip:review_ticket``, `sourcetype=cip:search_evidence`, `sourcetype=cip:search_evidence``.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=itsm` `sourcetype=cip:review_ticket`, `index=cip_evidence` `sourcetype=cip:search_evidence`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Emit HEC when the responsible manager signs the annual review. (2) Schedule the inventory recon search to write `cip:search_evidence` events. (3) Alert if `signed_ok=0` approaching the compliance date. (4) PDF the dashboard for the audit binder.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=cip:review_ticket review_type="CIP-002_Categorization" earliest=-400d
| eval signed_epoch=strptime(signed_at,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval annual_due=relative_time(now(),"-1y@d")
| eval signed_ok=if(signed_epoch>=annual_due,1,0)
| append [
    search index=cip_evidence sourcetype=cip:search_evidence search_name="CIP002_inventory_recon" earliest=-400d
    | stats latest(_time) as last_proof by search_name
  ]
| table review_type, owner, signed_at, signed_ok, last_proof
```

Understanding this SPL

**Annual CIP-002 Categorization Review Evidence Package (CIP-002-6 R4)** — We timestamp inventory validation searches and signed review workflows so annual categorization review evidence is searchable and complete.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=itsm` `sourcetype=cip:review_ticket`, `index=cip_evidence` `sourcetype=cip:search_evidence`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: cip:review_ticket. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype=cip:review_ticket, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **signed_epoch** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **annual_due** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **signed_ok** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Appends rows from a subsearch with `append`.

Output fields: annual_due, last_proof, owner, review_type, signed_at, signed_epoch, signed_ok.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the official BES or cyber-asset list, the ESP edge firewall, and the same Windows or network data this search is built to mirror for that time window. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Single value (review current), Timeline (signatures vs proof runs), Table (owners)

## SPL

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=cip:review_ticket review_type="CIP-002_Categorization" earliest=-400d
| eval signed_epoch=strptime(signed_at,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval annual_due=relative_time(now(),"-1y@d")
| eval signed_ok=if(signed_epoch>=annual_due,1,0)
| append [
    search index=cip_evidence sourcetype=cip:search_evidence search_name="CIP002_inventory_recon" earliest=-400d
    | stats latest(_time) as last_proof by search_name
  ]
| table review_type, owner, signed_at, signed_ok, last_proof
```
